prompt: A surreal, photorealistic diptych scene set in a large open landscape divided subtly into two distinct regions by lighting and terrain. In the left region, small groups of people are each climbing gentle slopes, clearly progressing upward with ease and confidence. Each group appears internally consistent, everyone moving in the same upward direction. In the right region, similar small groups are also climbing their own slopes, again progressing upward within their groups. However, when viewed as a whole from a higher perspective, the entire landscape forms a massive hidden downward slope, so that all groups together are actually descending into a vast valley. The global direction contradicts every local direction. The composition emphasizes this contradiction: locally upward movement everywhere, but globally a clear downward flow. Use natural terrain, realistic human movement, and cinematic lighting. The uncanny feeling comes from the realization that every small truth is correct, yet the overall truth is the opposite. Style: ultra-realistic, cinematic, high detail, natural colors, aerial perspective subtly revealing the hidden global structure. Negative prompt: text, numbers, charts, arrows, diagrams, symbols, mathematical notation.
visual_logic: Each group climbs upward locally, but the full landscape slopes downward globally, creating a reversal between subgroup trends and overall outcome.
